contenders.handicapper_notes: Third-up today and peaks here. Excellent heavy track profile (3: 0-1-0) and ran a solid fourth on heavy going at Donald last start. Simon Gebert stable is highly capable in regional country grade.

Strong second-up credentials. Ran a close second at Balaklava on heavy going last start. Handles wet tracks beautifully (2: 0-1-0 on heavy). Drawn wide in barrier 10 but possesses tactical early speed.

Fitter for a first-up fifth at Murray Bridge where he led before tiring. Sue & Jason Jaensch stable targets these regional fixtures. Anti-clockwise oval layout suits his on-pace style, though heavy track is a query.
Solid third first-up over 1100m on Soft. Sophie Logan riding from an ideal inside barrier (3). Tactical speed allows her to settle in the first four before turning for home.
First starter by Strasbourg for the powerful Michael Hickmott stable. Trialed nicely at Halidon and gets the benefit of the inside rail. Watch market closely.
First starter by Frosted debuting on his home track for Sue Murphy. Ran third in a jump-out at Mortlake. Local stable must be respected.
First starter by Atomic for local trainer Darryl Dodson. Fitter for a jump-out at Horsham. Best watched.
Led to the turn but faded badly on a wet track at Ballarat last start. Needs to show massive improvement to threaten.
First starter by Trust In A Gust. Late rider Kate Walters. Trials have been average; prefer to see one.
First starter by Rock Hero. Trials have been plain and has drawn the widest gate. Hard to recommend.
